took Uzziah, who was sixteen years old, and made him king in the room of his father Amaziah.

son of Amoz. According to the Jewish authority Kimchi, Amoz, not to be confused with the prophet Amos, was "the brother of Amaziah, the father of Uzziah, . . . [which] would make Isaiah of royal lineage and a cousin of Uzziah," king of Judah. (Donald W. Parry, Jay A. Parry, and Tina M. Peterson, Understanding Isaiah , p.8)
